content.problemStatement:
Every day, Mike goes to his job by a bus, where he buys a ticket. On the ticket, there is a letter-code that can be represented as a string of upper-case Latin letters.

Mike believes that the day will be successful in case **exactly two different letters** in the code alternate.  Otherwise, he believes that the day will be unlucky. Please see note section for formal definition of alternating code.

You are given a ticket code. Please determine, whether the day will be successful for Mike or not. Print "YES" or "NO" (without quotes) corresponding to the situation.
constraints: S consists only of upper-case Latin letters
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first and only line of each test case contains a single string S denoting the letter code on the ticket.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing 'YES' in case the day will be successful and 'NO' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first string 'ABABAB' has exactly two different letters 'A' and 'B' that alternate, so the output is 'YES'. The second string 'ABC' has three different letters 'A', 'B', and 'C' that do not alternate, so the output is 'NO'.
The string 'AAAAAA' has only one different letter 'A' that does not alternate, so the output is 'NO'.
The string 'AB' has exactly two different letters 'A' and 'B' that alternate, so the output is 'YES'.

solution.approach: To determine if the day will be successful, we need to check if the string S has exactly two different letters that alternate. We can do this by iterating over the string and checking if the current character is the same as the previous one or the one before that. If we find more than two different letters, we can immediately return 'NO'. If we finish iterating over the string and have found exactly two different letters that alternate, we return 'YES'. Otherwise, we return 'NO'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * |S|) — where T is the number of test cases and |S| is the length of the string S. This is because we are iterating over each character in each string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we are using a constant amount of space to store the current character and the previous two characters.
